title: dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. herbaceum
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. herbaceum is a subspecies of the plant Dorycnium pentaphyllum, commonly known as purple broom, a perennial flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, to treat conditions such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and arthritis. The health benefits of Dorycnium pentaphyllum subsp. herbaceum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve kidney function, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
